content.html: Priscilla King — The Herald and the Reset
The King Estate Morning

items: Priscilla begins her day inside the sprawling private gym complex of the King estate, racing her father in the Astralyn Viper through an indoor track built for spectacle.

She rolls a 12 on her Dexterity save and loses cleanly, her father teasing but proud, the competition sliding seamlessly into two full hours of training: lifting, stretching, drills, and dance practice.
The gym itself is more compound than workout space — dining rooms, bedrooms, showers, tunnels connecting structures — a world built to ensure she never leaves the spotlight.

content.html: The Herald at the Island Counter

items: After cleaning up, Priscilla and King sit at their polished island when the Herald appears silently and seats herself without invitation, sliding two coffees forward.
She is wafer-thin, almost brittle in silhouette, her skin placid and waxy. Her eyes reflect no light.
Silver restraints circle her wrists and neck — seamless, lockless, impossible to decipher.
King demands clarity. The Herald replies evenly: “I’m not here for you. I’m here for Priscilla.”
Priscilla rolls Insight (9). She believes the Herald likely does not mean harm, but certainty eludes her.

content.html: “What Are You Most Afraid Of?”

In this world, magic isn’t rare—it’s patented. Created. Bred. A century ago, Magi-Knights were guardians of the world. They saved it… and modern capitalism found them. Now their power is licensed, branded, medicated, insured, and televised.
content.caption: VoidDoll

content.html: What is Magi-Knights?

Magi-Knights Awakening is a 5e-based TTRPG that blends 90s magical girl/sentai anime (Sailor Moon, Power Rangers) with high school drama. Players balance student life with transforming into magical heroes to combat Spectral Outsiders using a Soul Crystal system. It features mechanics for managing social stress, school, and tactical, team-based combat.
The Players:

The Players are :
Heirs, enforcers, defectors, or experiments
Bound to one of “family-run” Magi Corporations
Publicly hailed as heroes
College Students
Privately owned assets
Theme/Flavor:

items: Found family vs inherited monsters
Romance under impossible pressure
Horror baked into legacies
“Strays” who don’t fit the system
Bloodline expectations
Parents who are terrifying legends
Power awakening late—or wrong
Cosmic consequences
Family loyalty vs moral horror
Corporate cover-ups
PR over lives
“Heroes” as controlled assets
Internal abuse protected by NDAs
Smear campaigns, hero rankings, monetized disasters

content.html: BackStory
Text text text
TRIGGER WARNING: Child abuse, drug abuse, sexual assault, mental abuse.

A young boy brought into this world by two tabaxi parents that supposedly loved him, but that’s getting too far ahead of ourselves. Ethereal Owl mainly known by Owl to his friends. He was well liked by all and very little had much disdain for him unless they were horribly petty and cruel. Ethereal learned how to play a multitude of instruments, clean, cook, hunt, and various other things his parents felt would be best for him to learn. He was a smart child to excelled in all of the things he did. The one thing he was not the best at was people who were deceptive since he had a very trusting nature and saw the best in the people.

Everything in Ethereal’s life  was going quite well till he was around twelve years old when his whole world would take a turn he wouldn’t see coming from a mile away when a dark skinned woman came to their home on which was on the outskirts of a forest that neighbored a darkened mountain that had an entrance Ethereal was never told to go due to monsters that lived there. The woman was very kind, yet very intimating to Ethereal. He was enthralled by the way she talked almost as if it were regal yet threatening like she had coming out of her mouth than words whenever she spoke. Even though he felt she was threatening she asked him many questions about his education, talents, and so on. She seemed very pleased and gave him words of affirmation and once her time had come to an end he heard her say, “This one is the best yet, he will do just fine since the last one died sadly…” As Ethereal asked what she meant by that he felt someone behind him grab his arms restraining him and a cloth over his mouth just as he was looking up to see his father drugging him with a cloth covered in chloroform. His eyes grew heavy and his mind began to float away into a dark abyss of unconsciousness.

It wasn’t till he woke up hours later surrounded by what felt like an everlasting darkness like a pool of ink that spilled into the sky and refused to release it from darkness. He began to panic asking for his mamma and poppa, but they never came to his cries regardless of how much he called out. As his eyes slowly adjusted he realized that a cart being pulled by carriage (horses or spiders pulling I don’t recall which in the Underdark really) he noticed several guards walking besides the carriage some in two in the front and two in the back while two more walked beside his cage that he was in shackled to a ball so he had limited movement. His entire being wanted to run from this situation, but he wasn’t sure to run were he to break free due to the unfamiliar area he was in. All he could see was darkness as he decided to lie down crying asking for his mother and father calling out for them repeatedly but they never came and slowly over time he began to run out of hope. The two guards outside laughed and mocked as the tabaxi child wept for his family. He had never experienced such a level of cruelty from anyone or anything in life, but he would soon find this cruelty was merely a meager sample size.

When he finally arrived to a town he saw lanterns that were most strange instead of the usual fire that he had seen around his everyday life they were of a blue fire lighting up a limited space of the entire city/town that he had finally arrived at. Once the cart had stopped he was picked up by a giant drow who began to take him to a large mansion on a hilltop that looked very distinct as if it were out of a novel he had read, but it looked far more oppressive with the darkness surrounding it with the limited purple fire light that light up the outside. He was taken into a large opening into a wide basement that that a wide assortment of instruments from flails to various knives and whips of all sorts. Several devices that looked very sinister which would only inflict pain upon anyone that was beset upon them. Ethereal began to scamper as he tried to escape to no avail due to his cage that he was in and the bonds that were around his hands made him feel like the had thousands of pounds added to his body. He was left alone for awhile when the giant drow set him down and left him to the darkness that felt like it consumed everything around him. Minutes felt like hours in that darkness till he heard the soft steps of someone in the distance. He could tell they were barefooted due to the pitter patter of the feet against what he imagined was cold stone smoothed out to a fine sheen. Then he saw a familiar face and he lit up with hope once again. It was the lady that had the soft and calm nature even though he feared her to a degree, but he felt like she was shinning in this darkness that enveloped them both. She was in a silky red dress that seemed to hide little, but he had no idea of practicality when it came to dress, but maybe this was just something casual for her to wear. She slowly walked to his cage with a key in her left hand and with each step his fears began to evaporate into joy as he would be freed by these dark skinned people and taken home soon.

As she opened the cage she had another to undo his chains to the ball that kept him from moving and he latched himself to her hugging her just saying in a hurried and grateful manner, “THANK YOU, THANK YOU, THANK YOU!!!” he began to sob realizing this had to be a mistake. He felt both arms wrap him up into an embrace as she nuzzled him like a mother would and she said, “Good boy, now I am your mother and you will do as I say, do you understand?” Ethereal merely nodded wiping away his tears as she set him upon a table that looked like it was stained with something, but reeked of copper which he had only smelled a handful of times. She began to make her way around the large slab of stone to have him like back and relax as she smiled at him like a mother would to a child she loved most dearly. She stepped away to a table grabbing a couple of things making clicking and clacking noises as she crushed something possibly in a mortar and pestle. Then he smelt burning which smelled off, but he trusted her that she knew what she was doing. She brought over the bowl which had everything in it now and asked him to smell and take deep breaths. As he did he felt his head go light almost numb as if everything began move as if everything blurred. He saw her smiling as she began to put restraints on his arms and legs. There was nothing he could do, he felt a wide range of things all at once arousal, ecstasy, euphoria as he his head began to spin. He felt her hands go up and down his body as he basked in the nature of his drugged state, but soon he felt the cold of the room itself. He clothes had been cut off with some shears and he was completely naked.

She herself began to undress and looked at him smiling, “I get to test the merchandise before letting anyone have a round with you at all. Try to enjoy yourself.” She began to violate the young child molesting him. Taking away the innocence only a child should know for several more years, but robbed from him in an instant. He felt in his mind it was wrong everything that was happening was just wrong, but the drugs would overwhelm that sensation and within an instant he would be back into the throes of drug in his system. This lasted several hours till what he imaged would be the morning, but he couldn’t tell. Once she had finished she moved over to a table with several instruments of torture and began the rigorous process of cutting him up like cattle. He would scream in pain, but she would merely give him more of the drug she had as she would edge him and torture him at the same time. When she had her fill she left him for what felt like several hours as he inevitable cried himself to sleep passing out due to sheer exhaustion. He would awaken several hours later propped up face down on the slab he slept on only to be awakened to barbed nine tails smacked across his skin. He groaned in agony as he looked about to see several people of various levels of affluence around him. The woman that had tormented him last night stood among them as she conversed with the nine tails in her hand. After sometime she called for everyone’s attention saying, “Good day my good men and women, I bring you today our newest specimen for you to sample and help me get into proper form. He’s been broken in a little, but he needs more of a hefty hand to help the process move along, so I urge you have your fun since you have paid in full and live out your greatest fantasy!” She merely walked to the back of the room and observed as the once again drugged child was molested repeatedly by many walks of life. Nobles, lowlifes, paladins, and even fellow drow had their way with him with both pain and pleasure unto the poor tabaxi boy.

By the end of the day he just felt numb and empty. He was covered in various fluids be it his own or others. He would just continue to lie on his stomach shell shocked by what had transpired. The woman who had let this happen had one of her slaves pick him up and take him upstairs to bathe him. He merely sat in the bath as he was scrubed clean, his fur matted with blood and open wounds that were cut deep. he blankly starred a thousand yards into the walls of the very fine mansion that he now lived in.  His “treatment” would continue for several days  till he was completely docile and would bend to the beck and call of his new mistress that rule his little world. He would learn to be charming and kind to all the guests at the brothel that he would be in with several other races that were left to the cruel fate that he was. Ethereal however was the furthest from being one to conform. over time he learned to to be sneaky and make several attempts to escape and punished a good measure each for his attempts. He would be tortured for several hours till his mistress deemed it was enough and would be cleaned off so his blood wouldn’t stain the hardwood floors.

For ten years Ethereal lived in the Underdark as a slave to this wretched woman of a drow servicing whomever wanted him that night or day. He was a rarity in the Underdark due to being tabaxi and most relished the idea to spend time with him. He hated every moment of it giving up on the hope of anyone coming for him ever. He gave into the darkness that had been around him for a little over a decade. One night after he had serviced someone and they had finally fallen asleep he began to quietly cry asking for help begging for anyone or anything to help him and that’s when he heard a whisper in the dark. “Do you wish to be free of this wretched and evil place young one?” Ethereal responded quietly, “Yes, I lack the strength to do it myself and wish to run far from here and I do not far for the cost in the slightest. I wished these monsters would all die…” There was a brief silence and the disembodied voice spoke again, “Good, then let me and I will take control and allow you to free yourself of this wretched fate that has befallen you, Ethereal. Ethereal nodded and agreed to the terms not knowing fully what he was accepting as a pact. He felt a force entire his body as if it was overtaking his entire being forcing it’s way into his very soul and he black out completely… In this state he ripped the throat of the noble next to him hearing tearing out his throat like a feral animal. As this force began to search the noble’s body he found a knife and that’s all he needed as he tore through the entire brothel cutting everyone down without discrimination or regard for who they were. Friend or foe mattered not only the will to satiate the blood lust of the dark urge itself in all it’s malice and bloody glory.

When Ethereal had woke from his slumber that he had finally taken he found himself covered in blood unsure of what had happened, but then he remembered the voice and what he agreed to. This was the price to pay for something sinister that he did not fully understand and instead of feeling shame he merely felt apathy as if he didn’t care who had died for his cause to escape that horrid hell hole. He was blinded by the sunlight his eyes having issues adjusting. but after a time he did and realized that he had wandered far from where he had been as several fae, changeling, and pixies watched him in curiosity as a bloodied tabaxi had entered their realm/woods. Ethereal knew of the fae wild and it’s folk and knew that trespassing usually meant death. He put his hands before him bowing begging for mercy that they let him live and allow him to leave, but one changeling and approached him and put a hand on his shoulder seeing through him almost as if he already knew his struggle and he began to cry again. The fae folk had a council and after several days agreed to allow him to live among them and teach him how to be of use to them and the outside world. He learned how to fight and proficient in many arts. Befriending many of the fae folk especially Pan a Changeling who was his good friend and Porxy who was a pixie that took a liking to Ethereal.

Among the fae folk he found his new family and a new home. He never forgot about the many years in the underdark. He always sat wrong with him that he never had the chance to kill his mistress, but that time would soon come and when he would he would be free and no longer a slave to her or anyone. This would be his vengeance.

items: King exits reluctantly after being told some words are “for the Magi Knight alone.”
The Herald asks, “What are you most afraid of?”
Priscilla hesitates, then answers honestly: losing her father.
The Herald follows: “And what will you do if your greatest fear comes to pass?”
Priscilla responds without hesitation: “Well, I’m a king, so I’ll stop it.”

The prophecy is delivered plainly:
“When the sun sets and the Azure night rises, a celebration will be held in the city center. There you will find destiny.”
As she leaves, claw-tipped fingers darkened at the ends, she commands: “Remember my words, Priscilla King.”

content.html: The Hour That Didn’t Happen

items: Priscilla pursues the Herald, paranoid for her father’s safety — but the Herald vanishes instantly.
She returns to find breakfast untouched and King speaking as if nothing occurred.
An hour has passed. Time has reset.
King reassures her that Hollow Vane and Fortune Veil sponsors monitor the Herald’s restraints and that they are “fully functioning.”
He gives her $1,000 in cash and a purple credit card for her first day.
Priscilla eats carefully, composed, but cannot shake the buzzing pressure in her chest — the taste of something wrong lingering in her throat.

content.html: Josef — Performance, Fabrication, and Fear of Replacement
The Agent Before Dawn

items: Josef is woken before five in the morning by his agent, who immediately informs him he must attend the Azure Night ceremony in full costume.
His bruises will be professionally covered. His image must remain pristine.
His quarterly ranking has fallen to fourth place or below. The board is displeased.

content.html: “We Kept You Relevant.”

items: The agent explains she fabricated a humanitarian narrative during his coma — building houses abroad, postcards, scripts — to preserve his brand.
He is enrolled at Blackmoat University under a retired Magi Knight for resume optics and market positioning.
The real threat is named: Priscilla King.
“To put it in layman terms, sweetie, we want you to be better than her.”

content.html: The Cost of Falling Behind

items: Josef must track her classes, trends, extracurriculars, and stay one step ahead.
If he fails, his prime contract is terminated.
He reacts immediately: “I don’t want that.”
He is handed a new phone, uniform, backpack, makeup, skincare, and eye drops for visible strain.
Both of his legs were broken. He has three hours before school.

content.html: Doubt Beneath the Smile

items: Left alone, Josef expresses genuine confusion. He feels like multiple narratives are being fed to him.
He does not know which version of events to trust.
He agrees anyway.

content.html: Arca — Innovation, Rivalry, and the White Suit Memory
Mina in the Archives

items: Arca and Mina begin in the Astralyn archives, where Mina loudly questions why he is doing homework before the first day of school.
She oscillates between wanting to fight Priscilla in a Wendy’s parking lot and calling her hair iconic.

content.html: The Pocket Roomba Demonstration

items: Mina unveils a six-inch “Pocket Roomba” modeled after the Astralyn Viper.
When fed pencil shavings, it emits a horrific grinding noise and smells like burnt wood and egg farts.
Mina declares it nearly perfect and gifts one to Arca despite his concern about school rules.

content.html: The Drive to Blackmoat

items: Mina bounces beside the hot pink Viper in the garage, thrilled.
Arca rolls Insight (11).
Unlike others, he clearly remembers the waking nightmare involving Vanessa Astralyn and the man in the white suit.
He is certain he will see them again.
He arrives at Blackmoat balancing sibling chaos and quiet dread.

content.html: Izeal — Sponsorship, Leverage, and the Casino
“Be Her Bestie.”

items: Izeal’s father berates him for being late and orders him to secure the Fortune Veil sponsorship by befriending Priscilla King within the first quarter.
“I want her eating out of your palm.”
Failure means his sister is transferred out of Lapis.

content.html: The Secondary Mission

items: Izeal demands resources after mentioning an attacker near his apartment.
His father agrees — but only if Izeal and his sister move back into the old casino.
He is assigned a new agent and shown documents referencing Lion Heart of Lux Crown Biomedical.
He receives a second objective: recruit the unaffiliated Magi Knight Charades.
Footage shows her fighting like a brawler, adored by Hollow Vane’s audience.

content.html: The Casino Floor

items: Izeal finds his sister laughing with blackjack employees after accidentally making a double entendre about “grabbing chips.”
A worker named Kelly asks for a photo of Priscilla for her child and gives him her number.
Izeal informs his sister they are moving back home. She visibly stresses but accepts.
He leaves carrying both ambition and obligation.

content.html: Betty — Charades, Rinky Drink, and Five Miles of Snow
The Win

items: Betty rolls Perception as exhaustion claws at her — but she feels awake.
She has just won $3,000 in an underground fight.
Her knees, knuckles, and jaw ache. Her eye is swelling. She feels triumphant anyway.

content.html: Rinky Dink and Viral Exposure

items: She lives in a condemned skate rink called Rinky Dink.
Her eel-like herald, later named Pastaroni, excitedly shows her a viral video of her Magi Knight form.
She rolls Perception (13) and realizes he secretly recorded and posted it.

content.html: Steven and the Plan

items: Steven, plush exterior and cigarette-stained tank top, admits he “might have fucked up.”
Hollow Vane has footage of her transformation. They froze distribution — but her face is known internally.

Steven outlines the plan:
Betty makes friends at Blackmoat.Those friends infiltrate Hollow Vane and steal a specific red-marked data stick.
Betty makes friends at Blackmoat.
Those friends infiltrate Hollow Vane and steal a specific red-marked data stick.
Backup plan: cause a distraction at Azure Night so Steven can infiltrate a broadcast van and remotely wipe the data.
They both acknowledge: “We’re so fucked.”

content.html: Shower. Uniform. Friends.

items: Betty showers in an industrial tub with a hose and a bleached mop bucket.
She repeats her mantra: “Shower. Uniform. Friends.”
The Blackmoat uniform is the softest fabric she has worn in years.
Blackmoat’s contracts are magically and legally unbreakable.
Her family cannot remove her.
For the first time in a long time, she feels hope.

content.html: The Walk

items: Public transit drops her short. She walks five miles in gentle snow.
Steven whispers from stuffed-animal form: “You’re motherfucking Charades.”

content.html: Josef Stops the Car

items: Josef rolls a natural 20 (18 total) and notices her walking alone.
He pulls over and offers her a ride politely.
She accepts softly, hiding her scarred hands in extended sleeves.
She introduces herself as Betty.

content.html: Blackmoat University — The Convergence
Siblings and Spectacle

items: Mina and Izeal’s sister immediately reconnect, animated and loud.
Mina loudly threatens to fight Priscilla in a Wendy’s parking lot before declaring her iconic.
The Astralyn Viper rolls up in shimmering spectacle.

content.html: The Bump

items: Priscilla steps out, poised and golden-haired.
A student bumps into her and continues walking.
She snaps at him.
He replies: “We’re all equal inside the academy,” punctuated with finger guns.

content.html: The Tug

items: In that moment, everyone except Priscilla feels it.
A metaphysical string.
A connection unmistakable to Magi Knights.
The student wears no uniform and carries a briefcase.
Someone runs up behind him and calls him “Professor.”

content.html: Bibiloplex
Josef Adderson grew up in a lower middle class family with two parents and two siblings.

The Addersons were your typical happy family that anyone would dream 
of falling into right out of birth. Sadly this joy would not last for 
long as the Addersons would be hit with Josef’s father would become 
diagnosed with cancer while Josef was in the middle of his teens. While 
caught early, it was very hard on the family as a whole due to Benjamin 
(Benny to his friends and family) was the sole means of income for the 
household due to his job being well paying in the current economy. While
 savings were kept in his back pocket for his family.

They
 were drained slowly but surely over a span of years due to the fact 
that treatments and bills were always coming in for Benny and the 
family’s utilities not including their needs of food or housing. Due to 
pure desperation Josef and his mother Daniella would both have to get 
jobs and try to cover the loss of money and need to pay any further 
bills for treatment as well as necessities for the family. Josef found 
basic minimum wage jobs and his mom found marginally better occupations 
but still couldn’t find anything that equaled the income that Benny 
brought home for his family. The situation grew more dire as years of 
treatments began to bleed so far into the family savings that it was no 
longer tenable anymore to work two jobs for Josef and his mother.

Caelan was born into pressure and silence, the son of the Duke of the
 Eighth Territory, a man as unyielding as the lands he ruled.
He never knew his mother.

Her
 name was never spoken in the halls. No portrait hung in her memory. No 
servant dared whisper of her. When Caelan was young, he would ask—softly
 at first, then with growing interest—who she was. Where was she now? 
His father’s answer was always the same:
“She is gone. That is all that matters.”
But Caelan dreamed of her.
As a rider.
Always on dragonback,
always just beyond reach.
There was another absence in the house—one spoken of only once.

His
 father, deep in his cups, told him of an older brother. A half-brother.
 Stolen from his cradle by a nursemaid before Caelan was even born. An 
investigation was launched, hunters sent, gold spent—but nothing was 
ever found. The child vanished as if swallowed by the earth itself.
After that night, the subject was buried.
But Caelan never forgot.

rows.value: Foolish Behavior that gets someone hurt

content.html: Josef Adderson grew up in a lower middle class family with two parents and two siblings.
Strixhaven Opening by Graciously Kait
Strixhaven Closing by NathanV
Early Life
Erymis was born to a large family. The only Tiefling among them.

Arrival and Meeting: The students tumble into an unknown area of Witherbloom and find themselves in a clearing, where they hear voices, they do not know who the voices belong to yet. The party makes brief introductions before combat begins.

Combat Begins: The ground rumbles and magic suffuses the clearing. A woad and two awakened vines appear. Some of the party members notice the strangeness of the woads and vines. They exhale putrid scent and vile green magic.

After Combat: August spots a kitsune and Cassius sees a fairy both quickly vanish from sight. The group gathers in the clearing and determines that they may be near the Detention Bog. Its possible moving that way will lead the newly formed party to help. Finnick decides to fly above the tree line to get a better idea where they are, spotting someone in the distance. He plays a pan flute, attracting their attention. The group agrees to head to the Detention Bog but needs to inform Finnick.

Meeting Apple: Before they can leave, a large Furbolg named Apple lands before the group, introducing himself as the older student in charge of them. Apple is concerned about the other students that the group has described seeing but proceeds to lead them to campus for orientation.

Return to Campus: The group travels back through a portal to campus. August throws up on arrival. Olivine Everhart, a professor, stops them and accompanies them to orientation. The group encounters Professor Rakesh, who recognizes Nhim.

Orientation and Encounters: The group tries to find the doctor at orientation. Olivine leaves to give a speech. Apple eventually locates the doctor. Olivine directs the students to choose their homeroom teacher. 

Session End with a terrifyingly intimidating presence arriving.
Early Life

Kaldrian Rivain Thornveil was born into a prestigious and influential family (The Thornveils) in a hidden Elven court. His mother, Lyralith, was a high-ranking Elf, known for her beauty and intelligence. Among her many official consorts, one stood out - Malkon, a Tiefling known for his charm, wit, and a unique talent of the tongue that delighted Lyralith. Despite the outcry from the pure-blooded Elves of council and the scandal of mixing bloodlines, Lyralith loved Malkon and treated him with great respect and affection. This unconventional union resulted in the birth of their son, Kaldrian, who inherited traits from both his Elven and Tiefling heritage.

Kaldrian’s relationship with his family was complex. His mother, Lyralith, was a stern but loving figure who saw great potential in him. She pushed him hard, instilling in him the values of cunning, strategy, and the importance of appearances. Malkon, his Tiefling father, was a supportive and loving parent who taught Kaldrian the importance of embracing his unique heritage and the power of resilience. Kaldrian also had an older sister, Eliandra, a pure-blooded Elf, and heir. She admired Kaldrian’s resourcefulness and cleverness. Despite the favouritism she received, she was protective and loyal as a sister.
School Life

Kaldrian grew up in privilege and prejudice. While he enjoyed the luxuries of a noble life, he also faced intense discrimination from his peers. His Tiefling traits - small horns, a strange glow to his skin, and a prehensile tail - made him a target for bullying and ostracism. Schoolmates often called him derogatory names, mocked his appearance, and played especially cruel pranks on him. Setting his tail on fire was a weekly occurrence.

The bullying was relentless. Kaladrian was often pushed, tripped, and his belongings destroyed… His peers would create elaborate schemes to humiliate him, such as using illusion magic to amplify his Tiefling features or spreading rumors about his infernal heritage. The teachers, bound by the biases of the Elven court, often turned a blind eye or subtly encouraged the mistreatment. Despite his father’s advice to embrace his uniqueness, Kaldrian struggled with feelings of isolation and anger. Demedicus’ little brother bullied Kaldrian often until Kaldrian snapped and then snapped his neck - he wasn’t dead until later - but the blame was still placed on Kaldrian.

Desperate to fit in and escape the torment, Kaldrian often tried to remove his tail or saw off his horns. These attempts at self-mutilation left him with scars on his tail, often accompanied by the residual burn marks from his classmates, and the tip of his tail disfigured. One of his horns is partially broken, a permanent reminder of his struggle to be accepted. Each time he hurt himself, his father Malkon, would find him and comfort him, reminding Kaldrian of his beautiful, unique strength, and the importance of embracing his heritage. Malkon’s love and support were the only things that kept Kaldrian from losing himself entirely to the despair of his differences.

Desperate to fit in and escape the torment, Kaldrian often tried to remove his tail or saw off his horns. These attempts at self-mutilation left him with scars on his tail, often accompanied by the residual burn marks from his classmates, and the tip of his tail disfigured. One of his horns is partially broken, a permanent reminder of his struggle to be accepted. Each time he hurt himself, his father Malkon, would find him and comfort him, reminding Kaldrian of his beautiful, unique strength, and the importance of embracing his heritage. Malkon’s love and support were the only things that kept Kaldrian from losing himself entirely to the despair of his differences.

To cope with the constant harassment, Kaldrian threw himself into his studies and training. He excelled at swordplay, archery, and the art of deception and diplomacy. His mother’s strict tutelage in court etiquette and political maneuvering prepared him for the treacherous environment of the Elven court. He learned to use his charm and intelligence to outwit his bullies, turning their schemes against them and earning grudging respect.

Ellelrith grew up with parents Cenda and Rashi who were devout followers of the Raven Queen. They'd take her with them to their weekly gatherings growing up in the religion. As she got older she started straying away from the Raven Queen finding kinship in the moon. Many nights she'd stay up late talking to the moon like an old friend. When she was old enough to leave home she searched for a sense of belonging. On her travels she would stay in cheap inns and talk with people in the bars until dawn. In the town of (insert town name) she met a group of adventurers traveling in search of something they all had lost. An unlikely group. At first glance you'd think they hated each other but the camaraderie was evident after they got settled. Elle had gotten into a deep conversation with their Cleric, a follower of Selune where she spoke about her parents and their devout belief in the Raven Queen. This Cleric, Grumblefoot Tumblewhisk, a halfling, started telling her about all the religions he had come across. Though he was very drunk Elle listened, soaking up every word seeing if there was something in there that called to her. After they parted ways she started going to temples of these gods Grumblefoot spoke of. For quite some time she searched for a calling something that would lead her to something bigger. Every night on her journeys as she laid in her tent she would speak to the moon like an old friend. She came across a temple to Selune, a cleric inside reciting prayer, speaking with the people. As she sat and looked at the cleric and the statue of Selune she saw a sparkle. A misdirection of light? Maybe she hadn't cleaned her eyes when she woke up this morning? She felt like she had to go up to the statue to speak to her. As she stood the cleric walked up to her, taking her hand and speaking "She calls to you to help those lost and afraid in the darkness." Then he simply walked away. What did that mean? As she stepped closer she felt a kinship with Selune. Later that day she went to a library to learn more about clerics of Selune and that night she started her journey to becoming one. Unknowingly she was already on the right track. Her late night talks with the moon never stopped but now instead of speaking to the moon she'd speak to Selune like a friend. It wasn't until a few years later as a new Twilight cleric that she met some adventurers who needed her help. She traveled with them on a mission to defeat some monsters in a cave. She barely knew the people but she could defend herself if needed right? That was until they left her for dead in that cave. Alone in the darkness she called out for help. Had it been the monsters in the cave that stabbed her or the adventurers she was with? Why had she been so dumb to blindly trust strangers with her safety? Elle crawled as far as she could before darkness overtook her. As she awoke she was staring at the moon and a fire crackled next to her. Was she on a bedroll? A man was sitting on the log next to the fire cooking up some soup. As she rose he looked up at her concern in his eyes. “Are you alright? I found you unconscious in the caves. What happened to you?” he asked. She took a moment to take him in. He was tall 6’5” maybe taller? He was wearing dark armor and carrying a greatsword. He had dark pink horns and a tail and light pink skin that darkened by dirt. She realized she had been staring for a while and never answered his question. “I was with an adventuring group sent to clear out the monsters. I don’t know what happened. I just remember being stabbed and they ran away and left me. I crawled as far as I could before I collapsed.” He stared at her eyebrows drawn downwards in concern. “What can you tell me of the group that left you to die?” Did he think she was stupid for trusting strangers? Was she supposed to pay him for helping her? Oh my gods she was being so rude. As she went to reach for her coin purse she realized it was gone. “They robbed me? I didn’t even have that much coin on me! Why would they do that?! Try to kill me just for some coin?!” At this point she was crying and on the verge of a panic attack. She heard him stand and make his way over. He put his hand on her shoulder and she flinched away. “ My name is Ermir. I pulled you from that cave and let you rest.I will not hurt you little one. You were hurt because you are too trusting. They will get what is coming to them. I can promise you that. Come sit and have some soup.” As she took his hand and followed him to the log she was able to really take in his height. Compared to him she must look tiny. Would he even take her seriously as a fighter? Sure she was a cleric but she could wield her warhammer just as well as any fighter. Then she was focused on him holding her hand. When was the last time she liked someone? When was the last time she let someone take care of her? By the time she realized she should pull her hand away they were sitting down. He handed her a bowl of soup and just smiled at her. She looked at him confused. “Why are you helping me? You don’t owe me anything. I can’t even repay you.” He just shrugged. “I’ve been in your position. Finding a purpose and wanting to help others only to be let down by those you wanted to help. You didn’t deserve what happened to you and I’m sorry that I was there too late to stop it.” She stared at him hard and placed a hand on his arm causing him to look at her. “You were not responsible for what these people did to me.You could not have known nor were you responsible for stopping it. We try to stop the injustice in the world and help people find their way through the dark but we can only do so much.” Her hand slips away from his arm and she hugs herself. “Sometimes we are just as lost as they are.” She’s staring at the moon with tears streaming down her face. He watches her like she’s a piece of living art. Sorrow in her eyes illuminated by the moon as if it was kissing her face. He clears his throat and goes to stand. “Get some rest. I’ll get you back to town in the morning and you can tell me what you remember of this party. I did find your pack and weapon but it seems they took your tent. You can share mine if you’d like. I have an extra bedroll.” She looks up at him and he’s blushing the cutest shade of red. “Sure. I think I’d feel safer that way.” She gets up, grabs her warhammer, and goes to lay down. He’s confused but just chuckles to himself and puts out the fire. The next morning they head back to town and talk about how they found their purpose. A mighty paladin for Selune meets a chosen cleric. When they make it to an inn he pays for a week's stay for her meals included. They chat over dinner about the party asking around to see if anyone remembers more about what they looked like. They shared the room that night. Only the accidental brush of hands. She craves his warmth but is afraid to ask for it. They wake up a tangle of limbs well rested. When they realize their positions they both clamber to stand embarrassed. They both apologize at the same time laughing as they speak over each other. Any easy friendship, maybe more? She’s never had more and definitely not with someone who looks like him. As he packs to look for this group he implores her to rest more. Before he leaves she rushes to give him a hug. For protecting her. For getting justice for her. Maybe also because she’ll miss his warmth under her cool touch. As she looks up at him trying to memorize his face he leans down and leaves the lightest of kisses on her lips before slipping away. While he’s gone she trains herself back up getting stronger. She takes on quests to stay at the inn and afford her meals and she waits for his return. Days turn into weeks which turn into months. It’s been 3 months since he left. What if he’s hurt? What if he’s lost? With this realization she packs up. She tells the innkeeper goodbye after asking for a general area he may have gone to. The Underdark is calling.

Adra was born into a close-knit tribe, deep within the heart of a druid grove that lay hidden in the wild lands of the shifter court. The woods surrounding the grove were infamous for being dangerous—a place where people often got lost, never to return. The dangers were real, but the druids had a tradition of finding these lost souls, offering them shelter, and guiding them safely out of the forest.ņ

The grove was like one big family, with all the love and squabbles that come with living so closely together. Adra was happy there, despite the occasional drama that comes with any family. The grove had a unique trait that set them apart: most of the people there were lycanthropes, able to shift into wolves. This wasn’t just a small quirk; it was something that made them famous—there were legends and rumors in the surrounding lands about the "wild wolf druids."

Even though they were known for their ability to transform, they tried to keep it hidden from outsiders. They didn’t want to scare off the people who sometimes wandered into their woods, lost and afraid. The grove had a reputation for taking in strays, offering sanctuary to anyone in need, even if they were from distant lands or different courts.

Adra grew up in this environment, where the line between the wild and the civilized was often blurred. She learned early on how to navigate the complexities of living in a community where everyone looked out for each other but also had their own secrets to keep. She knew the importance of protecting their way of life while still being kind to those who needed their help. For Adra, the grove wasn’t just home—it was a place where she learned what it meant to be part of something bigger, something that balanced the wilderness within with the need to care for others.
“All were equal, naked under the light of the moon in the Oak Father’s embrace”
Nightcycle

The first few days in the Underdark were a complete marvel for Adra, she simply couldn’t have enough of it. The bioluminescence, the flora and fauna were…everything she couldn’t possibly have imagined. Her small adventure was short-lived. Their camp was  found out by the local Drow. After they were brought into the drow city, her companion was killed right in front of her after a very swift and subjective trial. In shock and horror she transformed into her werewolf form to fight back, managing to kill one of the guards holding her, but she was alone. After Adra was subdued again instead of being killed she caught the interest of the queen herself, fascinated by her abilities to become a fuzzy toy not to mention she could take other forms as well. From this day on she became the family's pet, when she got bored of her she would throw Adra, for months at a time, into the fighting pits earning Adra many different battle scars.

For the first 10 years, Adra was even hopeful in a way that she would get out and find her way back home, to her pack, her family. But time passed. With great difficulty she even managed to get her hands on some acorns, she wanted to plant a tree, for Sylvanus to be able to find her here. The Seedling never survived.
